purpose: Defines the !plan {task} slash command — produces a detailed implementation plan WITHOUT writing any code. Reads context (TOC, CLAUDE.md, grep/glob, key files), designs the plan (per-change file/what/why/dependencies/risks), determines execution mode (Serial if ≤3 changes with deps, Parallel if 3+ independent changes), presents the plan with Context Summary / Execution Mode / Changes / Dependency Order ASCII tree / Cross-Layer Impact / Risks & Mitigations / Testing Strategy / Estimated Scope sections, then WAITS FOR USER APPROVAL (Proceed/Modify/Abort) before any execution. Read-only analysis.
gotchas: Do NOT write any code — this command produces a plan only. Approval-gated.
Be SPECIFIC: 'Update the file' is not a plan; 'Add a phone?: string field to UserProfile interface at line 15' is a plan.
Every plan must include a verification/testing strategy.
Do NOT begin execution until user explicitly approves — Proceed/Modify/Abort prompt is mandatory.
Mode determination is concrete: ≤3 changes with deps = Serial; 3+ independent = Parallel.
patterns: Plan-first development: gather → design → mode-decide → present → approve → (then execute).
conventions: Per-change template: File / What / Why / Depends on.
